rows.s1_tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · identity verification AT THE DOOR

rows.s1_lede: Taylor Cheney opens the state UI portal to submit their weekly certification. Because UI claims are fraud-sensitive — identity impersonation and duplicate claims across states are known fraud patterns — Maestro orchestrates identity verification at login itself. Biometric proof of identity established at the session level, before any certification action begins. Witness event #1.

_notes: fraud-fabric usecase shows a document in S1 — this flow should be CLEAR-at-the-door, not signing.

rows.s1_lede:
David Reyes, Procurement Officer at TGK, initiates a new vendor onboarding workflow in CLM for Fontara, Inc. — vendor profile, category, contract terms, and primary contact (Sarah Mitchell, Sales Rep) are entered.

When David submits the configuration, CLM detects the vendor type and auto-assembles the MSA + Vendor Onboarding Form + Insurance Certificate Acknowledgement — pre-populated from TGK's standard templates and sourcing data.

rows.label: procurement
rows.subtitle: × Fontara, Inc. — Annual MSA Renewal at Scale
rows.s1_tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · MAESTRO-TRIGGERED · RENEWAL ALERT FIRES
rows.s1_head: Maestro detects Fontara's MSA hitting the 60-day renewal window.
rows.s1_lede:
Maestro watches every active vendor agreement in TGK's CLM repository. When Fontara, Inc.'s MSA hits the 60-day renewal window, the workflow fires automatically — no calendar reminder, no manual chase. The renewal package assembles from the active contract terms, the previous year's submission, and any policy updates since.

Sarah Mitchell at Fontara receives the signing invitation in her inbox; David Reyes at TGK sees the renewal queued in his approval dashboard.
rows.b1_head: Maestro detects the 60-day renewal window.
_notes: Tenant "TGK" is not in the catalog — workflow type cannot be inferred.

From inside the deal room, the workflow fires the four-document package: loan agreement, security agreement, UCC-1, personal guaranty.

Maestro intercepts the portal login and invokes CLEAR — no separate IDV. Biometric proof bound to the session before any transaction.

The adaptive webform pre-fills from Meridian's CRM; Lloyd's selections drive the document package shape. Maestro fans out origination documents the moment Lloyd submits.

rows.label: banking
rows.subtitle: × TGK Holdings — Account Maintenance · Authorized Signer Roster Update
rows.s1_tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
rows.s1_head: Patrick logs into Meridian's commercial portal to add a signer to the facility.
rows.s1_lede:
Patrick Meyer, CEO and personal guarantor on TGK Holdings's $2.5M working-capital facility, opens Meridian's commercial portal to add his son Mark Meyer as a co-signer on the facility now that he's stepping into the COO role.

Authorized-signer changes on commercial accounts above $1M are a fraud-target action class — Meridian's policy steps up authentication at the session level. Maestro intercepts the portal login and invokes CLEAR. Biometric proof of the CFO lands before Patrick sees the change form.
rows.b1_head: Patrick opens Meridian's commercial portal.

Beneficiary changes are a fraud-target action class — Cedar's policy steps up authentication at the session level. Maestro intercepts the app login and invokes CLEAR. Biometric proof lands before Patrick sees the change form.

Student-record changes touch billing, mailings, and FERPA-protected directory data — Maple Ridge's policy is to step up authentication on any change to identity-adjacent fields. Maestro invokes CLEAR before the form opens.

rows.label: slgov
rows.subtitle: × 1420 Oak Street — Account Maintenance · Permit Modification Request

rows.s1_lede:
Mid-construction at 1420 Oak, Marissa Rivera and her contractor decided to add a small dormer to the addition. She opens Springfield's permit portal to request a permit modification covering the added scope, plan-check rerun, and fee delta.

Permit modifications can shift inspection sequence and fees — Springfield's policy is to step up authentication for any change to an active permit. Maestro invokes CLEAR before the form opens.

rows.label: slgov-311
rows.subtitle: × Maria Cortez — Follow-Up on Open Requests
rows.s1_tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
rows.s1_head: Maria logs into Greenhaven's 311 citizen portal; Maestro orchestrates light-touch CLEAR at login.
rows.s1_lede: Maria Cortez opens Greenhaven Public Works' 311 citizen portal to check on a previous pothole report and possibly add a follow-up. Multiple reports from the same citizen are legitimate, but duplicate fraud (fake updates to game response-time metrics) is a known 311 risk. Maestro orchestrates a light-touch CLEAR at login — biometric proof of identity tied to the citizen's service-request account. Witness event #1.
rows.b1_head: Maria hits the 311 citizen portal login.

rows.label: slgov-311
rows.subtitle: × Maria Cortez — Pothole Report via QR Code
rows.s1_tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE WEBFORM VIA QR
rows.s1_head: Maria scans a 311 QR code on-site and files the pothole report — no account needed.
rows.s1_lede: Maria Cortez walks past a pothole on Cedar Avenue and scans a 311 QR code on the nearest utility box. A mobile webform opens pre-tagged with the GPS location from the QR scan. Maria selects "pothole," adds a photo of the damage, and submits. Maria is the citizen reporter; no account creation required.
rows.b1_head: Maria scans the on-site 311 QR code.

rows.label: slgov-311
rows.subtitle: × Maria Cortez — Account Maintenance · Edit-in-Progress Request
rows.s1_tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · LIGHT-TOUCH AUTH
rows.s1_head: Maria logs into Greenhaven's 311 portal to add info to her open request.
rows.s1_lede:
After her morning commute, Maria realized she forgot to mention the pothole had widened overnight. She opens Greenhaven's 311 portal to add a follow-up photo and updated note to her open service request at 1124 Cedar Ave.

Even routine 311 edits route through light-touch identity step-up — Greenhaven authenticates returning citizens with device + email link rather than full CLEAR. Maestro orchestrates the lighter auth path.
rows.b1_head: Maria opens Greenhaven's 311 portal.

Yolanda Rivera lost her EBT card while moving. She opens Eastside HHS's benefits portal to request a replacement EBT card and have a temporary virtual card issued for groceries this week.

EBT card requests are fraud-target actions — Eastside's policy steps up authentication for any change to benefits delivery. Maestro invokes CLEAR before the form opens.

Two months into his role at Cascade County, Rod Blagovich just had his first child. He opens the employee portal to add his daughter to family health coverage and elect dependent care FSA — both within the 31-day life-event window.

Benefits enrollment changes outside open-enrollment require qualifying-event documentation — Cascade County's policy steps up authentication for any change to benefits elections. Maestro invokes CLEAR before the form opens.

Capacity changes can shift fire-code clearance and ABC scope — Northbrook's policy is to step up authentication for any amendment to an active license. Maestro invokes CLEAR before the form opens.

The intake form configures the sub-advisor package: firm profile, fee-share schedule, regulatory disclosures, and the signers on both sides. The moment Cathie submits, the workflow assembles the package dynamically and the rest of this story runs on its own.

rows.s1_lede: Mira Sundar, CFO of TGK Capital, opens the Hillside client portal. Instead of accepting just a password, Maestro orchestrates a CLEAR identity verification — biometric proof of identity established at the session level, before any transaction begins. Witness event #1.

rows.subtitle: × TGK Capital — IMA Webform Intake
rows.s1_tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM

rows.s1_lede: Cathie Woods, Head of Compliance at Hillside Advisors, opens the new-engagement webform and configures the engagement for TGK Capital: entity type (LLC), AUM tier ($100M+), discretionary management mandate, custodian (Charlton Schwab).

rows.label: wealth
rows.subtitle: × TGK Capital — Account Maintenance · Update Authorized Signer Roster
rows.s1_tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
rows.s1_head: Mira logs into Hillside's client portal to update TGK's authorized-signer roster.
rows.s1_lede:
After TGK's VP of Treasury moved on, Mira Sundar — CFO of TGK Capital — needs to add the replacement (Jeff Gundlach) as an authorized signer on the discretionary advisory account at Hillside.

Authorized-signer changes on corporate accounts above $50M AUM are a fraud-target action class — Hillside's policy steps up authentication at the session level and requires dual authorization. Maestro intercepts the portal login and invokes CLEAR. Biometric proof of the CFO lands before Mira sees the change form.
rows.b1_head: Mira opens the Hillside client portal.

rows.s1_lede:
Walter Buffet, account owner at the Buffet family's wealth relationship, opens Cypress Wealth's client portal to initiate a one-time IRA rollover-in from his former employer's 401(k) — $1.4M transfer.

Maestro intercepts the portal login and invokes CLEAR. Biometric proof of the account owner before any high-value investment instruction is authorized.

rows.s1_lede:
Reggie Adesanya, Senior Financial Advisor at Cypress Wealth, opens the discovery intake form to configure the Buffet family's multi-account portfolio — IRA rollover, joint taxable account, and new family trust.

The adaptive webform captures household structure, goals, and account intent. Maestro fans out customized account documents and trust-establishment papers the moment Reggie submits.
rows.b1_head: Reggie opens the discovery-config webform.

rows.subtitle: × Buffet Family — Account Maintenance · Update IRA Beneficiary
rows.s1_tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
rows.s1_head: Walter logs into Cypress's portal to update his IRA beneficiary after a life event.
rows.s1_lede:
After his sister Mae's passing, Walter Buffet wants to update the contingent beneficiary on his Cypress IRA — adding the family charitable foundation he and Esther established at year-end, alongside the grandchildren via the family trust.

Beneficiary changes on retirement accounts are a fraud-target action class — Cypress's policy is to step up authentication at the session level. Maestro intercepts the portal login and invokes CLEAR. Biometric proof of the account owner lands before Walter sees the change form.
rows.b1_head: Walter opens the Cypress client portal.
